statement.markdown: Implement the heapify insertion operation in a max heap. Given a max heap property, we need to insert an element at the end of the heap while maintaining the max heap property. The heap is represented as an array, where the root is at index 0. The size of the heap is n. The heapify insertion operation is performed by maintaining the max heap property.
statement.inputFormat: The input to the function includes a list of integers representing the heap and the size of the heap (n). The input is already represented as an array, and the function needs to insert an element at the end of the heap.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the updated heap after the insertion operation.
statement.notes: The heapify insertion operation involves comparing elements from the parent to the child node and swapping them if necessary. The max heap property is maintained by ensuring that the parent node is larger than its children.

examples.explanation: Insert 31 at the end of the heap

hints.text: Remember to maintain the max heap property after inserting the element

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The editorial solution involves using a recursive function to traverse the heap tree and maintain the max heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute forcing the insertion operation by comparing the inserted element to each node in the heap and swapping them if necessary.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive function to traverse the heap tree and maintain the max heap property. The function compares the inserted element to its parent node and swaps them if necessary.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the max heap property is maintained after the insertion operation.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the optimal solution
